speaker: Angel of the Lord
quote_text: Do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife.
prompt_context: In a dream, Joseph was told not to fear taking Mary because the child was from the Holy Spirit.
question: Who told Joseph in a dream not to be afraid to take Mary as his wife?

speaker: Herod
quote_text: Bring me word, so that I too may come and worship him.
prompt_context: After the magi arrived, the ruler of Judea sent them to Bethlehem with secret intentions.
question: Who told the magi to report back after finding the child in Bethlehem?

speaker: John the Baptist
quote_text: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.
prompt_context: In the wilderness of Judea, a preacher called people to prepare for God's reign.
question: Who called people in the wilderness to repent because the kingdom of heaven was near?

speaker: Voice from Heaven
quote_text: This is my Son, the Beloved.
prompt_context: After Jesus was baptized, a voice from heaven publicly identified him.
question: Who declared from heaven that Jesus was the beloved Son after his baptism?

speaker: Satan
quote_text: If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become bread.
prompt_context: During Jesus' fast in the wilderness, the tempter challenged him to use his power for himself.
question: Who challenged Jesus in the wilderness to turn stones into bread?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Follow me, and I will make you fish for people.
prompt_context: By the Sea of Galilee, Simon Peter and Andrew were called away from their nets.
question: Who told Simon Peter and Andrew that he would make them fish for people?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: You are the light of the world.
prompt_context: In the Sermon on the Mount, the speaker described his followers as visible and influential.
question: Who told his listeners in the Sermon on the Mount, "You are the light of the world"?

speaker: Centurion
quote_text: I am not worthy to have you come under my roof.
prompt_context: A Roman officer appealed for healing and expressed confidence that Jesus could heal by command alone.
question: Who told Jesus he was not worthy to have him come under his roof?

speaker: Demons
quote_text: What have you to do with us, Son of God?
prompt_context: In the country of the Gadarenes, evil spirits confronted Jesus before he cast them out.
question: Who cried out to Jesus in the region of the Gadarenes, calling him the Son of God?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Follow me.
prompt_context: At the tax booth, the teacher called Matthew away from his work.
question: Who told Matthew at the tax booth, "Follow me"?

speaker: Disciples of John the Baptist
quote_text: Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?
prompt_context: A question was brought to Jesus comparing his disciples with other religious groups.
question: Who asked Jesus why his disciples did not fast as often as others?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: I am sending you out like sheep into the midst of wolves.
prompt_context: While commissioning the twelve, the speaker warned them about danger and opposition.
question: Who warned the twelve that he was sending them out like sheep among wolves?

speaker: John the Baptist
quote_text: Are you the one who is to come?
prompt_context: From prison, a question was sent to Jesus through John's followers.
question: Who sent word to Jesus asking whether he was the one who was to come?

speaker: Pharisees
quote_text: Your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the sabbath.
prompt_context: After the disciples plucked grain on the sabbath, a religious group challenged Jesus.
question: Who objected to Jesus because his disciples were plucking grain on the sabbath?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Why do you speak to them in parables?
prompt_context: After Jesus taught the crowds by the sea, a close group asked him about his method.
question: Who asked Jesus why he spoke to the crowds in parables?

speaker: People of Nazareth
quote_text: Is not this the carpenter's son?
prompt_context: When Jesus taught in his hometown synagogue, local people reacted with skepticism about his background.
question: Who questioned whether Jesus was the carpenter's son when he taught in his hometown?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.
prompt_context: While walking on the sea toward the boat, the speaker calmed the frightened disciples.
question: Who told the terrified disciples on the water, "Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid"?

speaker: Canaanite Woman
quote_text: Even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table.
prompt_context: A Gentile woman responded humbly and boldly when Jesus tested her persistence.
question: Who answered Jesus by saying that even dogs eat the crumbs from their masters' table?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Who do you say that I am?
prompt_context: At Caesarea Philippi, after asking what others were saying about him, the teacher turned the question on his disciples.
question: Who asked the disciples at Caesarea Philippi who they said he was?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: God forbid it, Lord! This must never happen to you.
prompt_context: After Jesus predicted his suffering and death, one disciple rebuked him.
question: Who tried to stop Jesus after he spoke about his coming suffering and death?

speaker: Voice from the Cloud
quote_text: This is my Son, the Beloved; with him I am well pleased; listen to him!
prompt_context: On the mountain, while Peter was still speaking, a heavenly voice interrupted the moment.
question: Who spoke from the bright cloud at the transfiguration, telling the disciples to listen to Jesus?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Why could we not cast it out?
prompt_context: After Jesus healed a boy whom they had failed to help, the disciples asked privately about their failure.
question: Who asked Jesus privately why they had been unable to cast out the demon?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Unless you change and become like children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
prompt_context: When the disciples asked about greatness, the teacher answered by setting a child before them.
question: Who told the disciples they must become like children to enter the kingdom of heaven?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: How often should I forgive?
prompt_context: After Jesus taught about dealing with sin, one disciple asked about the limits of forgiveness.
question: Who asked Jesus how many times he should forgive a brother or sister who sins against him?

speaker: Rich Young Man
quote_text: What good deed must I do to have eternal life?
prompt_context: A wealthy young man approached Jesus with a question about inheriting life.
question: Who asked Jesus what good deed he must do to have eternal life?

speaker: Mother of James and John
quote_text: Declare that these two sons of mine will sit, one at your right hand and one at your left.
prompt_context: A mother approached Jesus asking for places of honor for her sons in his kingdom.
question: Who asked Jesus for her two sons to sit at his right and left in his kingdom?

speaker: Crowds
quote_text: Hosanna to the Son of David!
prompt_context: As Jesus entered Jerusalem, the people around him shouted messianic praise.
question: Who shouted "Hosanna to the Son of David" as Jesus entered Jerusalem?

speaker: Chief Priests and Elders
quote_text: By what authority are you doing these things?
prompt_context: In the temple courts, religious leaders confronted Jesus after his public actions and teaching.
question: Who challenged Jesus in the temple by asking by what authority he acted?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Give therefore to the emperor the things that are the emperor's.
prompt_context: After being asked about paying taxes to Caesar, the teacher answered with a famous distinction.
question: Who said to give to the emperor what belongs to the emperor and to God what belongs to God?

speaker: Sadducees
quote_text: In the resurrection, then, whose wife of the seven will she be?
prompt_context: A group that denied the resurrection tried to trap Jesus with a case about levirate marriage.
question: Who asked Jesus whose wife a woman would be in the resurrection after being married to seven brothers?

speaker: Lawyer
quote_text: Which commandment in the law is the greatest?
prompt_context: During a test question, a Pharisaic legal expert asked Jesus to identify the greatest commandment.
question: Who asked Jesus which commandment in the law was the greatest?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: You are like whitewashed tombs.
prompt_context: In a public denunciation, the speaker condemned religious hypocrisy with striking imagery.
question: Who compared the scribes and Pharisees to whitewashed tombs?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?
prompt_context: On the Mount of Olives, a private question followed Jesus' prediction about the temple.
question: Who privately asked Jesus about the sign of his coming and the end of the age?

speaker: Bridegroom
quote_text: Truly I tell you, I do not know you.
prompt_context: In the parable of the ten bridesmaids, a late group arrived after the door had been shut.
question: In the parable of the ten bridesmaids, who said, "I do not know you"?

speaker: Chief Priests
quote_text: They conspired to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
prompt_context: Before Passover, the leaders plotted secretly against Jesus, though they wanted to avoid a public uproar.
question: Who plotted to arrest Jesus secretly and kill him before the feast?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Surely not I, Lord?
prompt_context: At the supper, after Jesus announced that one of them would betray him, the table reacted one by one.
question: Who repeatedly asked Jesus, "Surely not I, Lord?" after he said one of them would betray him?

speaker: Judas
quote_text: Surely not I, Rabbi?
prompt_context: At the same meal, one disciple addressed Jesus differently while asking if he was the betrayer.
question: Who said to Jesus, "Surely not I, Rabbi?" at the supper?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: You will all become deserters because of me this night.
prompt_context: On the way to the Mount of Olives, the teacher warned his followers that they would scatter.
question: Who told the disciples that they would all fall away that night?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: Even though I must die with you, I will not deny you.
prompt_context: After Jesus predicted denial, one disciple insisted on his loyalty more strongly than the rest.
question: Who vowed to Jesus that he would not deny him even if he had to die with him?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me.
prompt_context: In Gethsemane, the speaker prayed in deep anguish while submitting to the Father's will.
question: Who prayed in Gethsemane that the cup might pass, yet asked for the Father's will to be done?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Friend, do what you are here to do.
prompt_context: At the moment of betrayal in the garden, Jesus addressed the one who approached him with a kiss.
question: Who said, "Friend, do what you are here to do" when Judas arrived in Gethsemane?

speaker: High Priest
quote_text: Tell us if you are the Messiah, the Son of God.
prompt_context: During the hearing before the council, the presiding religious leader directly questioned Jesus under oath.
question: Who directly asked Jesus whether he was the Messiah, the Son of God, at the hearing?

speaker: Servant Girl
quote_text: You also were with Jesus the Galilean.
prompt_context: While Peter sat outside in the courtyard, a young female accuser spoke first.
question: Who first told Peter in the courtyard that he had been with Jesus the Galilean?

speaker: Judas
quote_text: I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.
prompt_context: After seeing that Jesus was condemned, the betrayer returned the silver and confessed his guilt.
question: Who confessed, "I have sinned by betraying innocent blood"?

speaker: Chief Priests
quote_text: It is not lawful to put them into the treasury.
prompt_context: After Judas threw down the silver, the temple authorities debated what to do with the money.
question: Who said it was not lawful to put the returned silver into the treasury?

speaker: Pilate's Wife
quote_text: Have nothing to do with that innocent man.
prompt_context: While Pilate was seated on the judgment bench, a warning came to him because of a troubling dream.
question: Who sent Pilate a warning to have nothing to do with Jesus?

speaker: Crowd
quote_text: His blood be on us and on our children!
prompt_context: During the trial before Pilate, the assembled people answered in a dark and memorable way.
question: Who cried out, "His blood be on us and on our children"?

speaker: Soldiers
quote_text: Hail, King of the Jews!
prompt_context: After dressing Jesus in a robe and crown of thorns, the governor's soldiers mocked him.
question: Who mockingly hailed Jesus as King of the Jews after putting a crown of thorns on him?

speaker: Bystanders
quote_text: This man is calling for Elijah.
prompt_context: As Jesus cried out on the cross, some people nearby misunderstood what they heard.
question: Who said that Jesus was calling for Elijah while he was on the cross?

speaker: Angel of the Lord
quote_text: Do not be afraid.
prompt_context: At the empty tomb, a heavenly messenger spoke to the women and explained that Jesus had been raised.
question: Who told the women at the tomb not to be afraid and announced that Jesus had been raised?
